worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison's empirical measurement confirms Opus 4.7 uses 1.46x text tokens and up to 3x image tokens vs 4.6 at the same per-token price — concrete data your cost models need.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Critical follow-up: the 3x image token inflation only applies to high-resolution images; small images are cost-neutral, which changes the practical risk calculus.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick's framing — OpenAI could have kept reasoners secret and denied DeepSeek the blueprint — is the sharpest strategic read on AI openness decisions in this batch.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Claude Code reverse-engineered into a visual map of 512k lines and 1,900 files — useful if you're building on top of or competing with the agentic coding layer.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Manifest is an open-source model router that picks cheap vs. expensive models per request in <2ms, claiming 70% cost reduction for agent builders — worth evaluating for infra.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The '30-day roadmap is ngmi' take is blunt but the 87k views suggest it's resonating as a distribution/product-velocity signal in the current AI cycle.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: OpenAI WAU flat since February despite the 1B user goal is a meaningful distribution signal for anyone thinking about where growth is actually happening in the AI product market.

voices.why: Doing actual empirical token cost measurement on Opus 4.7 — rare quantitative signal in a feed full of hype.
Consistently provides strategic framing on AI industry decisions; the reasoner history thread is the most analytically useful non-technical post in this batch.
Sharp, contrarian takes on agent-native product strategy and distribution dynamics with real engagement behind them.

examples: @aakashgupta spam-posting viral science/finance/pop-culture facts with no AI product relevance
@RoundtableSpace crypto shilling (AAVE exploit, Polymarket trading bots, 'GM CT' engagement bait)
@DataChaz motivational quote posts and zero-context reposts
